skill.name: google-agents-cli-publish
skill.description: Guides publishing deployed Google ADK and A2A agents to Gemini Enterprise and managing Agent Registry entries with agents-cli and gcloud.
skill.summary: Publish deployed ADK and A2A agents to Gemini Enterprise and manage Agent Registry records.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the backtick and weak-crypto detections are Markdown formatting and terminology false positives. The real risk is operational: the skill guides cloud CLI commands that can register, update, or delete agent resources, and it references local deployment metadata and external agent card URLs. No prompt injection, malware intent, credential exfiltration, or hidden execution was found.

medium_findings.title: State-Changing Cloud CLI Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill provides agents-cli and gcloud commands that can register, update, list, or delete Gemini Enterprise and Agent Registry resources. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but users should review project IDs, app IDs, and delete operations before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced command blocks are explicit cloud management commands with state-changing effects. The behavior matches the skill purpose and shows no malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External Agent Card URL Fetching
medium_findings.description: A2A registration uses an agent card URL that the command fetches during registration. This network access is expected, but users should validate URLs and avoid untrusted endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation states the command fetches an agent card URL for A2A registration. This is a clear network behavior, but it is transparent and user supplied.

low_findings.title: Local Deployment Metadata Reads
low_findings.description: The skill instructs use of deployment_metadata.json and uv.lock for auto-detection and SDK compatibility checks. These local file reads are disclosed and scoped to expected project metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file names and purposes are documented directly in the skill. I found no evidence of secret harvesting or hidden file traversal.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptographic algorithm hits occur in Markdown code formatting, command examples, table text, and ADK or A2A terminology. They are not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill consists only of SKILL.md documentation, and the cited areas are prose, tables, or fenced shell examples. No Ruby source or cryptographic implementation exists in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples for agents-cli and gcloud. They are documentation examples, but the commands can affect real Google Cloud resources if copied and executed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fenced bash blocks are clearly visible and expected for a CLI skill. The risk comes from operational impact, not hidden execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Provided Network Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The A2A flow accepts an agent-card URL and fetches it during registration. Users should confirm the endpoint belongs to their deployed agent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoint behavior is explicitly documented. It is legitimate, but untrusted URLs could expose users to incorrect registration data.

content.value_statement: Publishing deployed agents requires correct Gemini Enterprise flags, metadata, and registration mode choices. This skill guides ADK and A2A registration with agents-cli and Agent Registry commands.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use ADK registration versus A2A registration.
Provides agents-cli publish examples for flags, environment variables, and interactive mode.
Describes auto-detection from deployment_metadata.json for Agent Runtime deployments.
Lists Gemini Enterprise publish flags and their environment variable alternatives.
Shows gcloud commands for listing, inspecting, updating, and deleting Agent Registry records.
Documents troubleshooting steps for SDK versions, missing IDs, permissions, and agent card fetch failures.
limitations: It does not deploy agents; deployment is handled by a separate deploy skill.
It assumes the target agent is already running and reachable.
It cannot verify Google Cloud permissions or resource IDs without access to the user environment.
It provides command guidance and does not replace Google Cloud access reviews.

use_cases.title: Register a production ADK agent
use_cases.description: Publish an already deployed Agent Runtime agent into a Gemini Enterprise app with the correct resource IDs and display metadata.

use_cases.title: Publish an A2A agent card
use_cases.description: Register an Agent-to-Agent service by using its agent card URL and the right deployment target settings.

use_cases.title: Maintain Agent Registry entries
use_cases.description: List, inspect, update, or remove registered agent service records across Google Cloud projects and locations.

prompt_templates.title: Publish an ADK agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a deployed Agent Runtime agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me publish my deployed ADK agent to Gemini Enterprise. Ask for any missing project, app, runtime, display name, and description values.

prompt_templates.title: Choose registration mode
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare ADK and A2A before running commands.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my deployment details and tell me whether I should use ADK or A2A registration for Gemini Enterprise.

prompt_templates.title: Build CI publish command
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a non-interactive command for automation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a non-interactive agents-cli publish command for CI using environment variables for the required Gemini Enterprise values.

prompt_templates.title: Audit registry operations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance of Agent Registry records.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me inspect and update Agent Registry records for my deployed agents. Include commands and safety checks before delete operations.

output_examples.input: I have an Agent Runtime ID and a Gemini Enterprise app ID. What should I run?
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for a display name and description, then provides a publish command using ADK registration and your resource IDs.

output_examples.input: My A2A registration cannot fetch the agent card.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the agent card URL, deployment target, authentication state, and Cloud Run invoker permissions before suggesting a retry.

output_examples.input: Show me how to remove an Agent Registry entry.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains the difference between deleting the runtime agent and deleting a manually registered Service resource.

best_practices: Confirm project IDs, app IDs, and locations before running publish or delete commands.
Use non-interactive flags or environment variables for CI pipelines.
Validate agent card URLs and Cloud Run permissions before A2A registration.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to deploy an agent from source code.
Do not publish against production resources without reviewing command targets.
Do not register agent card URLs from untrusted or unknown services.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy agents?
faq.answer: No. It covers publishing already deployed agents to Gemini Enterprise and managing registry records.

faq.question: Which registration modes are covered?
faq.answer: It covers ADK registration and A2A registration, including agent card URL requirements.

faq.question: Can it be used in CI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents non-interactive flags and environment variable usage for automation.

faq.question: Does it read local files?
faq.answer: It references deployment_metadata.json and uv.lock for documented auto-detection and compatibility checks.

faq.question: What Google Cloud permissions are needed?
faq.answer: The skill references Discovery Engine permissions and Agent Registry editor permissions for relevant operations.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with operational caution. The commands can modify cloud resources, so users should review targets before execution.
